resources.string: Get started by adding your first age verification credential
Private age verification using zero knowledge proofs
Continue using English as the app language
Change Language button, currently showing: %1$s. Tap to select a different language.
You can change this later in Settings
Would you like to customize accessibility features like text size, touch targets, or reduced motion?
Open accessibility settings to customize the app
You can change this later in Settings
We\'ve enabled verbose descriptions to help you navigate the app.
This app needs to download security components for zero knowledge proofs (approximately 87 MB).
Your wallet is ready for secure age verification
This enables zero knowledge proofs for private age verification
This is taking longer than expected
The setup might be stuck. Try restarting.
The app needs to restart. This sometimes happens on first launch.
Storage initialisation failed. Please restart the app.
Storage initialisation failed. Please close and restart the app.
Free up at least 65 MB and try again
Your age verification uses zero knowledge proofs. Your date of birth is never shared.
Your credential contains your age. Nothing else. Not your name, not your address, not your identity.\n\nIt proves you are over (or under) a certain age without revealing your date of birth.

You can get a credential through an app from a company that already knows your age. Your bank or super fund, for example. Because they have already verified your age, nothing needs to be shared again.\n\nAlternatively, visit a participating location with identification proving your date of birth. Your date of birth is sighted, used to create your credential, and then discarded. The issuer never sees your credential. Your identity is never tied to it.

If you care for someone who needs an age credential, you can get one for them and manage it on your device.

Visit a participating location together with identification proving their date of birth. When you scan the QR code, you will be asked whether this credential is for you or for someone you manage.

Add a nickname so you can tell their credentials apart.

An app trying to verify your age can send a challenge directly to your Provii wallet. You choose which credential to use. Upon successful verification, you will be taken back to the requesting app and be able to proceed as verified.

Alternatively, you may be presented with a QR code on another device, or a link in your mobile browser that goes straight to your wallet. There is also a short code option for accessibility.

No personal information is ever shared. Just a yes or no answer to whether you meet the age requirement.

Your first step is to get a credential through a participating app or at a location near you.\n\nFrom there, you can verify your age whenever it is needed, without sharing any personal information. You can also manage credentials for people you care for.
Choose how you would like to get your first credential
I have a QR code to scan
A verifier gave you a code after sighting your identification
I have a QR code to scan. A verifier gave you a code after sighting your identification.
Get a credential from your super, bank, telco, health insurer, or anyone who already knows your age
Find a participating app. Get a credential from your super, bank, telco, health insurer, or anyone who already knows your age.
Visit in person with your ID to get a credential
Find a location near you. Visit in person with your ID to get a credential.
Manage credentials for people you care for
I care for someone. Manage credentials for people you care for.

To get a managed credential for someone you care for, visit a participating location together. The verifier will sight their identification. When you scan the QR code, you will be asked whether this credential is for you or for someone you manage.
I already have a QR code
Your credential has expired and needs to be replaced
Your age credential has been securely stored and is ready to use
You can now prove your age privately
Tap the \'Verify Age\' button to scan verification QR (Quick Response) codes at participating venues.
Your age has been verified privately
You can now return to the site to continue.
Point camera at verification QR code
Hold steady until you hear a confirmation chime.
This may take a few seconds
We are preparing a private proof using your saved credential.
Check your internet connection and try again
Check back soon as more apps add support for Provii
Check back soon as more locations are added
Issue a test credential to your wallet so you can verify against it from a relying party demo.
Select your preferred language. The app will restart to apply changes.
The app will restart to apply this language.
If you skip, the app will use English by default. You can change the language later in Settings.
1. Use the search box to find your language
Your system language is highlighted at the top if supported.
You can change this later in Settings.
No languages found matching your search
Help and information about language selection
Skip language selection and use English by default
Using the test environment. Tap the version number five times to toggle.
Generate a sandbox credential to exercise verification flows without touching production data.
Use an exact date instead of the age-based default.
Give this credential a name so you can identify it in your wallet.
A nickname is required for managed credentials
This action removes the credential from your device. You can retrieve it again from the issuer if needed.
The proving key and supportive files will be deleted and downloaded again the next time you open the app.
Provii Wallet accessibility service provides enhanced support for users with disabilities. This includes automatic credential reading, guided navigation, and simplified interactions.
All accessibility preferences will return to their defaults.
Use OpenDyslexic font for improved readability
Allow more time for timed actions
Hide advanced options and reduce clutter
Ask for confirmation on important operations
Allow QR (Quick Response) codes to be typed instead of scanned
Enable speech recognition for text fields
Adjust space between lines of text
AAA recommendations: 1.5x line spacing, 2.0x paragraph spacing, and 0.12em letter spacing.
No automatic timeouts for AAA compliance
Play a confirmation sound after successful verification
Provide haptic feedback after successful verification
Sound respects your device\'s silent mode settings
Enter the email address registered for this officer

1. Enter your officer email
2. Tap Get Started
3. Enter the holder's date of birth
4. Touch your YubiKey to sign the attestation
Could not proceed. Please verify your officer email and try again.
Verify user\'s identity and issue a digital age credential

1. Check user's physical identification document
2. Enter their date of birth
3. Verify information matches exactly
4. Issue credential with attestation QR (Quick Response) code
5. User scans QR code to receive credential
I have sighted a valid government-issued identification
The Date of Birth on physical document is: %s
The Yubikey LED should be blinking
Your session will expire in %d seconds. Would you like to save your progress?
Warning: Your session will expire in %d seconds
Could not save your progress. Encrypted storage is unavailable. You can continue without saving.
This person is under 18. The credential will be issued as a managed credential requiring a parent or guardian.
I have sighted a valid government-issued identification
The Date of Birth on physical document is: %s
Scan the issuance QR (Quick Response) code
Center the QR code within the frame and hold steady.
This is a verification QR code. Please scan the credential issuance QR code.
Enter the 12-digit verification code shown on the verifier\'s screen.
Type the code shown on the issuer\'s screen.
Voice input requires microphone access. You can enable it from system settings.
Voice input is not available on this device
No speech detected. Please try again.
No speech heard. Please speak louder.
Voice input error. Please try again.
Camera permission required for QR (Quick Response) scanning
Grant camera permission to scan QR codes or enable manual entry from accessibility settings.
Camera permission is required to scan QR codes
Camera preview for scanning QR codes
No credential available. Please get a credential first.
You have multiple credentials that can be used for this verification. Please select one.
Connection timed out. Please try again.
Unable to connect. Please check your internet connection.
Secure connection failed. Please try again.
Security verification failed. Please try again.
Invalid input. Please check your information and try again.
Another operation is in progress. Please wait.
Age requirement not met for this credential.
Biometric authentication is required to continue.
Wallet not initialized. Please restart the app.
Invalid data format. Please try again.
Storage error. Please check device storage.
Network error. Please check your connection.
Your date of birth is never shared. Verifiers only learn if you meet their age requirement.
Control data collection and manage your data
Provii Wallet uses zero knowledge proofs to verify your age without revealing your date of birth. Your credential data is stored only on your device and never shared with anyone.
Help improve Provii Wallet by sharing anonymous usage data
Help us fix bugs by automatically sending crash reports
Tap to learn about our data practices

- Anonymous app usage statistics
- Crash reports and error logs
- Device type and OS version
- App version information

- Your date of birth
- Your name or personal details
- Your location
- Your credential data
- Biometric data
Your credential data is never shared with Provii or any third party. It stays on your device.
Remove all stored credentials and data
This will permanently delete all your credentials and app data from this device. You will need to obtain a new credential to use the app again.
You can change these settings at any time. Changes take effect immediately.
Privacy settings, control data collection and manage your data
Attestation QR code for user to scan
Simpler words and shorter sentences (AAA compliant)
Get help with app features and accessibility
Browse help topics and learn about app features
No help topics found matching your search
Learn how to obtain your first age verification credential

To get your age verification credential:

1. Visit an authorised issuer location
2. Present your government-issued ID
3. Scan the QR code provided
4. Your credential will be saved securely

Your date of birth is stored locally and never shared.
How to prove your age without sharing personal information

When you need to verify your age:

1. The verifier will show you a QR code
2. Scan it with the app
3. The app creates a mathematical proof
4. Only the yes/no answer is shared

Your actual date of birth is NEVER revealed.
Customize the app for your needs

The app includes many accessibility features:

- Large text and high contrast modes
- Voice input and screen reader support
- Simplified gestures and user interface
- Manual code entry options
- Extended timeouts

Access these in Settings > Accessibility

Your privacy is our top priority:

- Credentials stored locally on your device
- Zero-knowledge proofs protect your data
- No tracking or analytics
- Biometric authentication available
- Data never leaves your device unencrypted

Common problems and fixes:

QR Scanner Not Working:
- Check camera permissions in Settings
- Try manual code entry option

Credential Not Loading:
- Check your internet connection
- Try resetting the proving key

Verification Failed:
- Ensure credential is not expired
- Contact the verifier for support

Try searching for:
• Accessibility
• Settings
• Help
• Language
This app uses the following open source software. We are grateful to the developers who created these libraries.
No internet connection. Please check your network and try again.
An unexpected error occurred. Please restart the app.
An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
Not eligible. You do not meet the requirements for this verification.
Challenge expired or not found. Please request a new one.
This challenge has expired. Please scan a new QR code.
Request out of order. Please try again.
Too many requests. Please wait a moment and try again.
Server error. Please try again later.
Please complete the steps in order
Authentication cancelled. Biometric authentication is required to use your credential.
Invalid data format. Please try again.
Storage error. Please check device storage.
Network error. Please check your connection.
Failed to generate proof. Please try again.
No internet connection. Please check your network.
This may take a few seconds
Your age has been verified privately
You can now return to the site to continue.
Please enter a verification code. The code should be either a 12-digit short code or a full verification URL from the verifier.
Add a credential for someone you care for, such as a child.
Are you sure you want to delete this credential? This action cannot be undone.
You have reached the maximum number of credentials. Delete an existing credential to add a new one.
You are about to verify age using %1$s\'s managed credential.
Your child\'s credential has been securely stored.
Are you sure you want to delete this managed credential for %1$s? This action cannot be undone. You will need to visit an issuer again to replace it.
This will delete your credential and any managed credentials for children. This action cannot be undone.
No credential found. Please set up your credential first.
No credential was selected. Please try again.
Multiple credentials are available. Choose which one to use for this verification.

1. Check user's physical identification document
2. Enter their date of birth
3. Verify information matches exactly
4. Issue credential with attestation QR (Quick Response) code
5. User scans QR code to receive credential
User should scan this QR code with their Provii Wallet app
The credential will be generated on their device
User will complete credential generation on their device
Network error - please check your connection
This may take a few seconds
Your age verification credential has been securely stored
Enter a nickname so you can tell credentials apart
Choose who this credential is for
Enter a nickname for the child
Please verify document and Date of Birth match
Not authenticated. Please enter your officer ID first.
Hardcoded fallback registry for sandbox environment
Official Provii sandbox issuer for testing
Birth date cannot be in the future
You are 1 step away from developer mode
You are %1$d steps away from developer mode
Timeout waiting for Yubikey touch. Please touch the key when it blinks.
Challenge-response not configured on this Yubikey slot
Slot 2 is not configured for HMAC (Hash-based Message Authentication Code) SHA1 challenge-response
HMAC (Hash-based Message Authentication Code) calculation failed: %s
Proving key not found - please re-download
Out of memory - please restart the app
Failed to update verifier URL (web address)! Expected %1$s but got %2$s
Proving key not available. Setup required.
Enable sandbox mode to generate test credentials
Cannot fetch sandbox credentials. Please check internet connection.

Control the contrast ratio between text and backgrounds:

• Standard: Default app colors
• High (AA): 4.5:1 contrast ratio
• Maximum (AAA): 7:1+ contrast ratio

Higher contrast makes text easier to read, especially in bright environments or for users with low vision.

WCAG AAA compliance requires Maximum contrast.

Increases all text sizes by 50% throughout the app.

This makes text easier to read for users with:
• Low vision
• Age-related vision changes
• Reading difficulties

Combines with system font size settings for maximum flexibility.

Removes transparent overlays and blur effects.

Benefits:
• Clearer visual hierarchy
• Better text readability
• Reduced visual noise
• Lower cognitive load

Recommended for users with low vision or cognitive difficulties.

Adjusts colors for different types of color blindness:

• Protanopia: Red-blind
• Deuteranopia: Green-blind
• Tritanopia: Blue-blind
• Monochrome: Complete color blindness

Each mode uses carefully selected colors that are distinguishable for that specific type of color vision deficiency.

Adjusts spacing between lines of text (1.0x - 2.0x).

• Default: 1.0x
• AAA Compliant: 1.5x or higher

Increased line spacing:
• Improves reading comprehension
• Reduces eye strain
• Helps users with dyslexia
• Makes text blocks less overwhelming

Adjusts spacing between paragraphs (1.0x - 3.0x).

• Default: 1.0x
• AAA Compliant: 2.0x or higher

Increased paragraph spacing:
• Clearly separates ideas
• Improves visual organisation
• Reduces cognitive load
• Helps maintain reading position

Adjusts spacing between letters (0.0em - 0.2em).

• Default: 0.0em
• AAA Compliant: 0.12em or higher

Increased letter spacing:
• Makes individual letters more distinct
• Helps users with dyslexia
• Improves reading accuracy
• Reduces letter confusion

Limits line length for easier reading:

• Full Width: No limit
• Comfortable: 80 characters (recommended)
• Narrow: 60 characters

Research shows optimal reading occurs at 60-80 characters per line. Shorter lines reduce eye movement and improve comprehension.

Increases minimum touch target size to 60dp (from 44dp).

Benefits:
• Easier to tap buttons and controls
• Reduces accidental taps
• Helps users with motor impairments
• Better for one-handed use

WCAG AAA requires 60dp minimum.

Removes all animations and transitions.

Benefits:
• Prevents motion sickness
• Reduces distraction
• Lowers cognitive load
• Improves focus

Recommended for users with:
• Vestibular disorders
• Attention difficulties
• Motion sensitivity

Controls automatic timeout on verification screens:

• No Timeout (AAA): Unlimited time
• Standard: 30 seconds
• Extended: 60 seconds

WCAG AAA requires no timeout option for users who need more time to complete tasks.

Form data is automatically saved before timeouts.

Replaces complex gestures with simple alternatives:

• Swipe → Tap button
• Long press → Regular tap with confirmation
• Pinch/zoom → Buttons

Helps users with:
• Motor impairments
• Tremor
• Limited dexterity
• Touchscreen difficulties

Provides vibration feedback for actions.

Benefits:
• Confirms button presses
• Indicates errors
• Provides tactile confirmation

Can be disabled for users who:
• Find it distracting
• Have sensory sensitivities
• Prefer silent operation

Automatically saves form data before timeouts.

Features:
• Warning 10 seconds before timeout
• Option to save or discard data
• Automatic restoration on return
• 24-hour data retention

Ensures no data loss if you need extra time or experience interruptions.

Reduces visual complexity:

• Fewer User Interface elements shown at once
• Larger, clearer buttons
• More whitespace
• Simplified layouts

Benefits:
• Easier to understand
• Less overwhelming
• Better focus
• Reduced cognitive load

Shows progress through multi-step processes.

Displays:
• Current step number
• Total number of steps
• Progress visualization

Benefits:
• Reduces anxiety
• Sets clear expectations
• Helps maintain orientation
• Shows completion progress

Provides extra context and explanations:

• Longer button labels
• More detailed instructions
• Additional hints
• Clearer error messages

Helps users:
• Understand actions before taking them
• Avoid mistakes
• Learn app functionality
• Build confidence

Requires confirmation for important actions:

• Deleting credentials
• Changing settings
• Clearing data

WCAG AAA requires confirmation for all destructive actions.

Benefits:
• Prevents accidents
• Allows review before action
• Reduces anxiety
• Provides second chance

Uses simpler language (Grade 7-9 level):

• Shorter sentences
• Common words instead of jargon
• Clear, direct language
• Fewer technical terms

WCAG AAA compliant when enabled.

Benefits:
• Easier to understand
• Faster comprehension
• Better for non-native speakers
• Reduced cognitive load

Context-sensitive help available throughout the app.

Features:
• 27 help topics
• Searchable help center
• Related topics navigation
• Technical glossary

Access help from:
• Settings screens
• Complex features
• Error messages
• Anywhere you see a help icon

Enter QR code data manually instead of scanning.

Benefits:
• Works when camera access is unavailable
• Helps users with motor difficulties
• Better in low light
• No need to hold phone steady

Alternative to camera-based QR scanning.

Use voice to enter text instead of typing.

Benefits:
• Hands-free input
• Faster than typing
• Helps users with motor impairments
• Reduces repetitive strain

Uses your device's built-in voice recognition.

Full support for TalkBack screen reader.

Features:
• All UI elements labeled
• Proper heading structure
• Logical navigation order
• Custom hints for complex actions

The app automatically enables:
• Detailed descriptions
• Enhanced touch targets

when TalkBack is detected.

Pre-configured accessibility profiles:

• Vision Impaired: High contrast, large text, haptics
• Motor Impaired: Large targets, extended timeouts, confirmations
• Cognitive: Simplified UI, step indicators, descriptions
• Elderly: Large text, targets, simplified UI, reduced motion
• Default: Reset all settings

Each profile enables the most helpful settings for that user group. You can further customize after applying a profile.
Please wait while we securely verify your age using zero knowledge proofs. Your date of birth is never shared with the verifier.
Point your camera at the verification QR code. The code will be scanned automatically when detected.
Your age has been verified successfully. The verifier knows you meet their age requirement without learning your actual date of birth.
Verification failed. Please try scanning the QR code again or use manual code entry.
Scan the QR code provided by the authorised issuer to receive your digital age credential.
Point your camera at the issuance QR code shown by the officer. Your credential will be generated on your device.
Credential successfully created. You can now use this credential for age verification.
A credential is a cryptographically signed proof of your identity that enables privacy preserving verification.
Zero-knowledge proofs allow you to prove you meet an age requirement without revealing your actual age.
Downloading cryptographic proving key required for zero knowledge proof generation
No credential available. Please obtain a credential from an authorised issuer first.
Network connection failed. Please check your internet connection and try again.
Invalid QR code. Please ensure you\'re scanning the correct verification code.
Verification failed. The server was unable to validate your proof.
Enter the applicant\'s verified date of birth from their official identification document.
Welcome to Provii Wallet - your privacy preserving digital age verification solution.
Your date of birth is stored securely on your device and never transmitted to verifiers.
To get started, visit an authorised issuer to obtain your digital age credential.
We\'re checking your age. We won\'t see or share your birthday.
Point your camera at the QR code. It will scan by itself.
Success! The website knows you\'re old enough. They don\'t know your birthday.
That didn\'t work. Try scanning again or type the code instead.
Scan the code from the government office to get your digital ID.
Point your camera at the code. Your ID will be saved on your phone.
Done! You can now use your digital ID to prove your age.
A credential is like a digital ID card that proves who you are without sharing private details.
You can prove you\'re old enough without telling anyone your real age.
Downloading security files needed for the app to work
You don\'t have an ID yet. Visit a government office to get one first.
Can\'t connect to internet. Check your wifi or data and try again.
That code doesn\'t look right. Make sure you\'re scanning the right code.
That didn\'t work. The website couldn\'t check your proof. Try again.
Enter the person\'s birthday from their official identification.
Welcome to Provii Wallet - prove your age privately.
Your birthday stays on your phone. Nobody else can see it.
To start, visit a government office to get your digital ID.
Please wait while this operation completes
These navigation keys work throughout the app with external keyboards
External Bluetooth and USB keyboards are fully supported
Use arrow keys to navigate between items in lists
Hold Ctrl to see available shortcuts on this screen
All features in this app are accessible via keyboard
Dismiss the current screen or dialog
Move focus to the next interactive element
Move focus to the previous interactive element
Activate the focused button or submit a form
Activate the currently focused button or toggle
Dialog opened. Press Escape to dismiss, Enter to confirm.
Double tap to confirm, or press Enter
Double tap to cancel, or press Escape
Double tap to perform destructive action
This app requires a screen lock (PIN, pattern, or biometric) to securely store credentials. Please set one up in your device security settings before continuing.
A screen lock (PIN, pattern, or biometric) is required to securely store credentials. Please set one up in your device settings.
Point your camera at the QR code shown by the officer
This QR code does not contain attestation data
This is a verification QR, not a credential QR
This QR code is for getting a new credential, not for age verification. Would you like to go to the attestation screen instead?
You already have a personal credential. Creating a new one will replace it. This cannot be undone.
This deep-link will only succeed if you enable sandbox mode in this wallet.
Sandbox link detected. Choose whether to enable sandbox mode and open the link.
This challenge was issued by a sandbox verifier. It will not succeed unless you enable sandbox mode in this wallet.
Sandbox challenge received. Choose whether to enable sandbox mode and continue, or reject the challenge.
Sandbox mode is now active. The wallet is connected to sandbox servers.

A security issue has been detected on this device. Credential access is restricted to protect your data. Please ensure the device is not rooted, no instrumentation frameworks are running, and the application has not been tampered with. Restart the app after resolving the issue.
A way to prove something is true without revealing the actual information.
A digital proof of your identity stored on your device.
A trusted authority that creates and signs your credential.
A website or service that checks if you meet an age requirement.
A square barcode that contains information you can scan with your camera.
A mathematical way to hide information while still allowing proofs about it.
A proof that a number falls within a certain range without revealing the number.
A request from a verifier asking you to prove something about your age.
A way to commit to information that can be revealed and verified later.
A mathematical proof that a document came from a specific source and hasn\'t been altered.
Technology that protects your personal information while still allowing verification.
Cryptographic data needed to generate zero knowledge proofs.
Cryptographic data used by verifiers to check proofs.
A single age verification attempt from start to finish.
Encryption that allows mathematical operations on encrypted data.
An organisation authorised to verify identities and issue credentials.

A zero knowledge proof is a cryptographic method where one party (the prover) can prove to another party (the verifier) that a statement is true, without revealing any information beyond the validity of the statement itself.

Example: You can prove you're over 21 without showing your exact age or date of birth.

This is the core technology that makes Provii Wallet privacy preserving. When you verify your age, the verifier learns only that you meet their requirement (e.g., "over 18"), not your actual age or date of birth.

A credential is a cryptographically signed piece of information that proves an attribute about you. In Provii Wallet, your credential contains your date of birth in an encrypted form.

Key properties:
• Stored only on your device
• Never transmitted to verifiers
• Used to generate zero knowledge proofs
• Issued by trusted authorities
• Cannot be forged or modified

Think of it as a digital version of your ID card, but one that allows you to prove specific facts (like "over 18") without showing the whole card.

An issuer is an organisation authorised to verify your identity and create digital credentials. This could be a government agency, DMV office, or other trusted institution.

The issuer's role:
• Verifies your identity in person
• Checks official documents (passport, driver's licence)
• Creates your digital credential
• Signs it cryptographically
• Helps you store it on your device

The issuer only needs to verify you once. After that, you can use your credential to verify your age as many times as needed without involving the issuer again.

A verifier is any website, app, or service that needs to confirm you meet an age requirement. Examples include:
• Websites selling age-restricted products
• Online services with age requirements
• Apps with age-gated content

What the verifier learns:
• Only whether you meet their requirement (yes/no)
• Nothing about your actual age
• Nothing about your date of birth
• Nothing that could identify you

The verifier receives a cryptographic proof that is mathematically valid but reveals no personal information beyond the yes/no answer to their specific age check.

A QR (Quick Response) code is a two-dimensional barcode that can store text, numbers, or web addresses. Provii Wallet uses QR codes in two ways:

1. Credential Issuance: Scan a code from the issuer to receive your credential
2. Age Verification: Scan a code from a verifier to start the verification process

QR codes are just a convenient way to transfer small amounts of data. If you have difficulty scanning QR codes, you can use the Manual Code Entry alternative input method to type the information instead.

A Pedersen commitment is a cryptographic technique that allows you to commit to a value without revealing it. It's like putting a number in a sealed envelope.

Properties:
• Hiding: No one can see what number is committed
• Binding: You can't change the number after committing
• Homomorphic: Allows mathematical operations on committed values

In Provii Wallet, your date of birth is stored as a Pedersen commitment. This allows you to prove facts about your age (like "over 18") without ever revealing your actual birth date.

A range proof is a type of zero knowledge proof that demonstrates a committed value lies within a specific range, without revealing the actual value.

Example: Proving you're between 18 and 120 years old without revealing you're 25.

How Provii Wallet uses range proofs:
• To prove your age exceeds a minimum (e.g., 18, 21)
• Without revealing your exact age
• Without revealing your date of birth
• In a way that's mathematically verifiable

The verifier can be 100% certain the proof is valid, but learns nothing about your actual age beyond the yes/no answer.

A challenge is a request issued by a verifier that specifies:
• What needs to be proven (e.g., "over 18")
• A unique session identifier
• A cryptographic nonce (random number) for security

The challenge ensures:
• Each verification is unique and can't be reused
• The proof is tied to this specific verification session
• Replay attacks are prevented
• The verifier can trust the response

When you scan a QR code from a verifier, you're receiving a challenge. Your wallet then generates a zero knowledge proof in response to that challenge.

A cryptographic commitment is like a sealed envelope - you can commit to a value without revealing it, and later prove what that value was without the ability to change it.

Two key properties:
1. Hiding: The commitment doesn't reveal the value
2. Binding: You can't change the value after committing

Your age credential uses commitments to hide your date of birth while still allowing you to prove facts about your age. The commitment is created when you receive your credential from the issuer.

A digital signature is a cryptographic technique that proves:
• Who created or authorised a document
• The document hasn't been modified since signing
• The signer cannot deny having signed it

In Provii Wallet:
• The issuer digitally signs your credential
• This proves the credential is authentic
• It prevents anyone from creating fake credentials
• It ensures your credential hasn't been tampered with

Digital signatures are the digital equivalent of handwritten signatures, but much more secure and verifiable.

Privacy preserving means the system is designed from the ground up to minimise data exposure and protect your personal information.

Provii Wallet's privacy preserving features:
• Zero-knowledge proofs reveal only yes/no answers
• Your date of birth never leaves your device
• Verifiers learn nothing about your actual age
• No tracking or profiling is possible
• No central database of verifications
• Each verification is unlinkable to others

Traditional age verification systems require you to show your full ID or date of birth. Privacy preserving systems like Provii Wallet allow verification without revealing unnecessary information.

A proving key is public cryptographic data used to generate zero knowledge proofs. It's like a recipe that tells your device how to create valid proofs.

Key facts:
• Public and safe to download
• Same for all users
• Required to generate proofs
• About 150-200MB in size
• Downloaded once and cached

The app downloads the proving key the first time you perform a verification. This is a one-time download that enables all future verifications.

A verification key is public cryptographic data that verifiers use to check if a zero knowledge proof is valid. It's the counterpart to the proving key.

Key facts:
• Much smaller than proving key (~1KB)
• Public and widely distributed
• Verifiers have their own copy
• Ensures only valid proofs are accepted
• Same verification key for all users

When a verifier checks your proof, they use the verification key to mathematically confirm it's valid without learning anything about your actual age.

A session represents one complete age verification interaction:

1. Verifier creates a challenge
2. You scan the QR code
3. App generates a proof
4. Proof is submitted to verifier
5. Verifier checks proof and responds

Each session:
• Is unique and time-limited
• Has a unique identifier
• Cannot be reused or replayed
• Typically completes in seconds
• Leaves no persistent record

Sessions ensure security and prevent someone from reusing an old proof to bypass verification.

Homomorphic encryption is a special form of encryption that allows certain mathematical operations to be performed on encrypted data without decrypting it first.

Example: You can add two encrypted numbers and get an encrypted result that, when decrypted, equals the sum of the original numbers.

Provii Wallet uses homomorphic properties of Pedersen commitments to:
• Compare encrypted ages to threshold values
• Generate range proofs
• Perform age checks without revealing age

This is what enables zero knowledge proofs to work - you can prove relationships between values without revealing the values themselves.

A trusted authority is an organisation that:
• Has legal authority to verify identities
• Follows strict identity verification procedures
• Is trusted by verifiers to issue accurate credentials
• Maintains high security standards

Examples:
• Government agencies
• Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV)
• Passport offices
• Licensed identity verification services

The trust model ensures that:
• Only legitimate authorities can issue credentials
• Verifiers can trust the credentials are accurate
• Users have confidence in the system
• Fraud is prevented

You must visit a trusted authority in person to receive your initial credential.
